title: COUNT(1) vs SUM of partial counts
poc: select sum(a) from (select count(1) a, k1 from test.t4 group by 2) as b;

insert into test.t4 (k1,k2,f) SELECT (SELECT 1+max(k1) from test.t4) + k1,k2,f
from test.t4;
key_bug_features: COUNT(1) query performance on large tables
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test.t5 (id INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT, value DECIMAL(10,2), description TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, PRIMARY KEY (id));
INSERT INTO test.t5 (value, description) VALUES (NULL, 'Test entry 1'), (123.45, 'Test entry 2'), (678.90, 'Test entry 3');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test.t5 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT SUM(value) FROM test.t5 WHERE value > 100.00;
